data.meta_description: Escape on a yoga paddleboard yacht Marbella charter from Puerto Banús. Flow on deck, SUP in Cala del Faro, all with included drinks & skipper.
data.summary: Combine yoga and paddleboarding on a private yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Our yoga paddleboard yacht Marbella experience includes a morning flow on deck, SUP exploration in calm coves, and a full day of luxury with skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Private yacht charter from Puerto Banús (Marbella)
Includes sunrise or morning yoga session on deck (mat provided)
Paddleboard (SUP) included for exploring Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or Cabopino
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT (21%)
Drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava; light snacks provided
Yoga suitable for all levels; instructor available on request (add-on)
Choose from 8- to 12-pax yachts like Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Mangusta 80 (from €4,719/4h)
Ideal for groups, wellness retreats, or a mindful day on the Costa del Sol

faq.q: Do I need to bring my own yoga mat?
faq.a: No, we provide a non-slip yoga mat for each guest. The deck is spacious and stable, especially on our flybridge yachts like the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) or Mangusta 80 (24m), giving you plenty of room to flow.

Yes, one stand-up paddleboard (SUP) is included on every yoga paddleboard yacht charter. You can use it in calm coves like Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or off Cabopino. Additional boards can be arranged on request.

faq.q: Can I book a yoga instructor?
faq.a: Absolutely. We can arrange a certified yoga instructor for your charter at an additional cost. Just let us know when you book via WhatsApp. Alternatively, you can lead your own session — the deck is your studio.

faq.q: What is the best time of day for yoga on the yacht?
faq.a: Sunrise or early morning (around 8:00–10:00) is ideal for a calm sea, soft light, and cooler temperatures. We can schedule your departure from Puerto Banús accordingly. Afternoon flows work too, especially with a breeze.

faq.q: Which yachts are best for yoga and paddleboarding?
faq.a: Larger yachts with wide flybridges or sundecks, like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), offer ample space. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a great option. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish VAT (21%), water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. The yoga mat and SUP are included at no extra cost. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A yachts.

faq.q: Can we combine yoga with other activities?
faq.a: Yes. After your yoga and paddleboarding, you can swim, snorkel, sunbathe, or cruise along the coast to Estepona or Sotogrande. Many groups anchor for a relaxed lunch. The skipper will tailor the itinerary to your preferences.

faq.q: How do I book the yoga paddleboard yacht experience?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. Let us know your preferred date, group size, and whether you’d like a yoga instructor. We’ll confirm your charter and send all details. Book early, especially in peak season.

data.body_html:
Combine the serenity of yoga with the freedom of paddleboarding on a private yoga paddleboard yacht Marbella charter. Departing from Puerto Banús, this experience lets you flow on deck as the sun rises over La Concha, then explore the Costa del Sol’s hidden coves on a stand-up paddleboard. With a licensed skipper, all drinks, snacks, and equipment included, it’s a full day of mindful luxury on the Mediterranean.

What is the Yoga & Paddleboard Yacht Experience?

This is a private yacht charter designed for wellness and exploration. Your day begins with a guided or self-led yoga session on the spacious deck of your chosen yacht. After stretching and breathing in the sea air, you’ll anchor in a calm cove — often Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés — and take out the included paddleboard to glide across crystal-clear water. The rest of the charter is yours to swim, sunbathe, or cruise along the coast. All charters include a skipper, fuel, insurance, drinks, and snacks. Yoga mats and a SUP are provided at no extra cost.

Why Choose a Yoga & Paddleboard Charter in Marbella?

Marbella’s coastline offers sheltered coves, calm morning seas, and stunning views of the Sierra Blanca. A private yacht gives you exclusive access to spots like Cala del Faro, Cabopino, or the quiet waters off Estepona — far from crowded beaches. The combination of yoga and paddleboarding is perfect for groups seeking a unique wellness day, whether you’re celebrating a birthday, hosting a retreat, or simply escaping the resort. With our all-inclusive pricing, there are no hidden costs. Just show up, flow, and paddle.

Our Yachts for Wellness Charters

We offer a range of yachts suitable for yoga and paddleboarding, all departing from Puerto Banús. For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) provides a vast flybridge and sundeck. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a stable platform with plenty of space. For smaller parties, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a nimble, affordable option. All yachts have easy water access for paddleboarding. See our full fleet of boats in Marbella for more details.

Pricing & What’s Included

All our wellness charters include the following at no extra cost:

  • Licensed skipper and fuel
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks
  • Insurance and Spanish VAT (21%)
  • Yoga mat and one stand-up paddleboard

Pricing varies by yacht. For example, the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 (Tier A) start at €749 for 2 hours, up to €2,299 for 8 hours. The Mangusta 80 (Tier B) requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 and includes a jet ski. For other yachts, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp. Contact us to discuss your ideal wellness itinerary.

Sample Itinerary: A Morning Flow & Paddle

Here’s what a typical 4-hour yoga paddleboard charter looks like:

  • 08:30 – Meet at Puerto Banús. Board your yacht and meet the skipper.
  • 09:00 – Depart towards Cala del Faro. Enjoy a gentle cruise with drinks.
  • 09:30 – Anchor in a calm cove. Set up your mat on the flybridge for a 45-minute yoga flow.
  • 10:30 – Take the SUP out to explore the coastline. Swim and relax.
  • 11:30 – Cruise back, with time for photos and a final drink.
  • 12:30 – Return to Puerto Banús.

Longer charters (6–8 hours) allow for more paddleboarding, a stop for lunch, or a visit to Sotogrande or Estepona.

Best Coves for Paddleboarding Near Marbella

The Costa del Sol has several calm, scenic coves perfect for SUP. Our skippers know the best spots depending on wind and sea conditions:

  • Cala del Faro – Sheltered, with clear water and rocky cliffs. Ideal for beginners.
  • Cala Cortés – A quiet inlet near Cabopino, often with minimal boat traffic.
  • Cabopino – A sandy cove with dunes and a marina. Good for longer paddles.
  • Estepona coast – Calmer in the afternoon, with views of the mountains.

Your skipper will choose the best location based on conditions and your preferences.

How to Book Your Yoga & Paddleboard Yacht Charter

Ready to book? Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, group size, and whether you’d like a yoga instructor. We’ll confirm availability and send a quote. For Tier A yachts, pricing is fixed; for others, we’ll provide a custom price. All bookings require a deposit. We recommend booking at least one week in advance, especially during summer (June–September). Start your wellness journey today.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need to bring my own yoga mat?

No, we provide a non-slip yoga mat for each guest. The deck is spacious and stable, especially on our flybridge yachts like the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) or Mangusta 80 (24m), giving you plenty of room to flow.

Is paddleboarding equipment included?

Yes, one stand-up paddleboard (SUP) is included on every yoga paddleboard yacht charter. You can use it in calm coves like Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or off Cabopino. Additional boards can be arranged on request.

Can I book a yoga instructor?

Absolutely. We can arrange a certified yoga instructor for your charter at an additional cost. Just let us know when you book via WhatsApp. Alternatively, you can lead your own session — the deck is your studio.

What is the best time of day for yoga on the yacht?

Sunrise or early morning (around 8:00–10:00) is ideal for a calm sea, soft light, and cooler temperatures. We can schedule your departure from Puerto Banús accordingly. Afternoon flows work too, especially with a breeze.

Which yachts are best for yoga and paddleboarding?

Larger yachts with wide flybridges or sundecks, like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), offer ample space. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is a great option. All depart from Puerto Banús.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish VAT (21%), water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. The yoga mat and SUP are included at no extra cost. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A yachts.

Can we combine yoga with other activities?

Yes. After your yoga and paddleboarding, you can swim, snorkel, sunbathe, or cruise along the coast to Estepona or Sotogrande. Many groups anchor for a relaxed lunch. The skipper will tailor the itinerary to your preferences.

How do I book the yoga paddleboard yacht experience?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. Let us know your preferred date, group size, and whether you’d like a yoga instructor. We’ll confirm your charter and send all details. Book early, especially in peak season.
